# Fitness Tracking Mobile App Brief

## Project Overview
Develop a mobile fitness tracking application that helps users monitor their workouts, nutrition, and health metrics with AI-powered recommendations.

## Business Context
- Company: "FitLife Pro" - health and wellness startup
- Current product: Basic web-based fitness tracker
- Target market: Health-conscious individuals aged 18-45
- Revenue model: Freemium subscription ($9.99/month premium)

## Problem Statement
Current limitations:
- Web-only platform, poor mobile experience
- No AI-powered personalization
- Limited workout tracking capabilities
- No social features for motivation
- Poor user retention (30% churn rate)

## Goals
1. Launch native mobile apps (iOS and Android)
2. Implement AI-powered workout recommendations
3. Add social features for community engagement
4. Improve user retention to < 15% churn
5. Reach 100,000 active users in first year

## Key Features Required
- Workout tracking (cardio, strength, flexibility)
- Nutrition logging with barcode scanning
- AI-powered workout recommendations
- Progress tracking and analytics
- Social features (challenges, leaderboards, friends)
- Integration with wearables (Apple Watch, Fitbit)
- Video workout library
- Personalized meal plans
- Gamification elements (badges, streaks)
- Push notifications for reminders

## Technical Considerations
- Cross-platform development (React Native or Flutter)
- Offline-first architecture
- Integration with health APIs (Apple Health, Google Fit)
- Machine learning for recommendations
- Real-time sync across devices
- Push notification system
- In-app purchases for premium features

## Constraints
- Budget: $150,000 for development
- Timeline: 4 months to MVP launch
- Team: 3 developers, 1 designer, 1 ML engineer
- Must support iOS 14+ and Android 10+
- App size < 50MB
- Battery-efficient (background tracking)

## Success Metrics
- 100,000 downloads in first 6 months
- 20% free-to-premium conversion rate
- < 15% monthly churn rate
- 4.5+ App Store rating
- Average session duration: 10+ minutes

## Risks
- App store approval delays
- Privacy concerns with health data
- ML model accuracy for recommendations
- Competition from established apps (MyFitnessPal, Strava)
- User onboarding complexity
